title: Trump’s returns shed light on tax offsets, foreign accounts
Trump’s taxes: Takeaways from release of long-sought returns
shortDescription: In one of its last acts under Democratic control, the House of Representatives on Friday released six years of former President Donald Trump's tax returns, dating to 2015, the year he announced his presidential bid.

title: Black support for GOP ticked up in this year’s midterms
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — Black voters have been a steady foundation for Democratic candidates for decades, but that support appeared to show a few cracks in this year’s elections.

title: Arizona governor’s tenure defined by push right, Trump feud
shortDescription: PHOENIX (AP) — Arizona Gov. Doug Ducey beamed as an excavator’s heavy claw smashed through the windows of an old state office building and began tearing off the façade. In one of his last public appearances in mid-December, the outgoing Republican governor watched the physical manifestation of a project that has defined his eight-year tenure: tearing down state government.

title: Ukraine’s debts: US aims to get IMF to reexamine loan fees
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — A provision in the recently signed defense spending bill mandates that the United States work to ease Ukraine’s debt burden at the International Monetary Fund, which could create tensions at the world’s lender-of-last-resort over one of its biggest borrowers.

title: Ginni Thomas says she regrets post-election texts to Meadows
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — Virginia Thomas, the wife of U.S. Supreme Court Justice Clarence Thomas, says she regrets sending texts to then-White House chief of staff Mark Meadows after the 2020 election, telling the House Jan.

title: AG: Meadows won’t face voter fraud charges in North Carolina
shortDescription: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — Mark Meadows, the former White House chief of staff to President Donald Trump, won't face voter fraud charges related to his 2020 registration and absentee vote in North Carolina, the state's attorney general announced Friday.

title: Biden pardons 6 convicted of murder, drug, alcohol crimes
shortDescription: KINGSHILL, U.S. Virgin Islands (AP) — President Joe Biden has pardoned six people who have served out sentences after convictions on a murder charge and drug- and alcohol-related crimes, including an 80-year-old woman convicted of killing her abusive husband about a half-century ago and a man who pleaded guilty to using a telephone for a cocaine transaction in the 1970s.

title: Jan. 6 takeaways: Final revelations from investigation
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — Destroyed documents. Suggestions of pardoning violent rioters. Quiet talks among cabinet officials about whether then-President Donald Trump should be removed from office. Interview transcripts released by House investigators in recent days — more than 100 so far — give further insight into the Jan.

title: Afghan refugees in US face uncertainty as legislation stalls
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — Congress has failed so far to create a path to residency for Afghans who worked alongside U.S.

title: Congresswoman wants explanation on Twin Cities mail slowdown
shortDescription: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — A Minnesota congresswoman wants to know what's causing days-long delays in mail delivery in the Twin Cities' southern suburbs.

title: Appeals court rules against transgender man in bathroom case
shortDescription: MIAMI (AP) — A federal appeals court has ruled that a Florida school district's policy of separating school bathrooms based on biological sex is constitutional.The 11th U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals announced its 7-4 decision on Friday, ruling that the St.

title: Afghan war orphan remains with Marine accused of abduction

title: Taxes fall, wages rise and jaywalking OK’d by new state laws
shortDescription: Taxes will fall and minimum wages rise for residents in numerous states as a variety of new laws take effect Sunday that could impact people's finances and, in some cases, their personal liberties.

title: Taxes fall, wages rise and jaywalking OK’d by new state laws
shortDescription: Taxes will fall and minimum wages rise for residents in numerous states as a variety of new laws take effect Sunday that could impact people's finances and, in some cases, their personal liberties.

title: Queen guitarist, women’s soccer team top UK honors list
shortDescription: LONDON (AP) — Those at the forefront of the U.K.'s response to Russia's war in Ukraine joined Queen guitarist Brian May and a fashion designer dubbed “the mother of the miniskirt” on the country’s New Year's Honors list on Friday.

title: Biden makes 1st public appearances during St. Croix vacation
shortDescription: CHRISTIANSTED, U.S. Virgin Islands (AP) — After two days out of sight while vacationing in the U.S. Virgin Islands, President Joe Biden ventured out Friday for a round of golf and dinner with his wife and grandchildren.

title: Court: Abortion doctors can’t be charged under Arizona law
shortDescription: PHOENIX (AP) — An Arizona court has ruled that abortion doctors cannot be prosecuted under a pre-statehood law that criminalizes nearly all abortions yet was barred from being enforced for decades.

title: N. Carolina doesn’t pick electric mix to lower carbon levels
shortDescription: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — North Carolina utility regulators told Duke Energy Corp. on Friday to carry out a series of activities to generate electricity that they say will help ensure greenhouse gas reductions set in a new state law are met.

title: Venezuelan opposition strips Guaidó of ‘presidential’ role
shortDescription: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — For three years, Juan Guaidó led the Venezuelan opposition’s efforts to bring about new elections and remove socialist President Nicolás Maduro.But on Friday, dozens of politicians who once backed Guaidó voted in favor of removing the 39-year-old engineer and replacing his U.S.-supported “interim government” with a committee to oversee presidential primaries next year and protect the nation’s assets abroad.

title: FEC levies $30K penalty tied to Kobach’s 2020 US Senate bid
shortDescription: TOPEKA, Kan. (AP) — Incoming Kansas Attorney General Kris Kobach agreed to help pay a $30,000 penalty to resolve a federal complaint over the improper use of a group's email list during his unsuccessful 2020 campaign for the U.S.

title: Candidates sign up for Jan. 31 races for state House, Senate
shortDescription: ATLANTA (AP) — Three candidates have signed up for a Jan. 31 special election to fill a vacant state Senate seat in southwest Georgia, while a Republican county administrator will run unopposed for an open seat in the state House.

title: Attorney General files intent to appeal judge’s bail ruling
shortDescription: SPRINGFIELD, Ill. (AP) — Illinois Attorney General Kwame Raoul on Friday notified the state Supreme Court that he will appeal a local judge's ruling that eliminating cash bail for criminal defendants is unconstitutional.

title: NY Rep.-elect Santos admits lying about career during election bid

title: GOP stumbles with independents contributed to midterm election outcome
shortDescription: Republican House candidates nationwide won the support of 38% of independent voters in last month’s midterm elections, according to AP VoteCast.

title: In Child Welfare Cases, Most of Your Constitutional Rights Don’t Apply
shortDescription: The child welfare system rarely offers the same rights as the criminal justice system, leaving many families facing permanent separation without due process protections.

title: Patients Went to This Isolated Facility for Treatment. Instead, Nearly Two Dozen Were Charged With Crimes.
shortDescription: For years, residents at the Illinois facility received scant treatment for their developmental disabilities and mental illnesses, then faced felonies for lashing out at staff.

title: The IRS Hasn’t Released Nearly Half a Million Nonprofit Tax Records
shortDescription: If you’re trying to evaluate a charity this year, you might have a hard time. The IRS is behind on releasing Form 990s, limiting access to key financial information the public uses to evaluate the nation’s tax-exempt companies.
